items.title: Huijsen speelde maanden met blessure: 'Kon niet eens vol sprinten'
items.summary: Dean Huijsen heeft een boekje opengedaan over zijn fysieke situatie. De verdediger werd de afgelopen periode bekritiseerd om zijn mindere spel. Nu blijkt dat hij de afgelopen maanden met een blessure heeft gespeeld.

items.title: Man City in de problemen na blamage bij Bodø: 'Het voelt alsof alles misgaat'
items.summary: Door de verrassende nederlaag op bezoek bij Bodø/Glimt moet Manchester City in de laatste speelronde van de Champions League alles in het werk stellen om de tussenronde te ontlopen. Wat is er aan de hand met de ploeg van Pep Guardiola? 'Bodø heeft City niet alleen verslagen, maar ook vernederd.'

items.title: 'Dronkenlap' Bellingham verklaart opmerkelijk juichgebaar
items.summary: Jude Bellingham heeft tekst en uitleg gegeven bij het juichgebaar dat hij maakte na zijn goal in de Champions League-wedstrijd tussen Real Madrid en AS Monaco. De Engelse middenvelder maakte een 'drinkgebaar' en refereerde daarmee aan mensen die hem 'een dronkenlap' noemden.

items.title: Union Saint-Gilloise’s Christian Burgess: ‘I’ll definitely be asking Harry Kane for his shirt’
items.summary: The former Portsmouth defender took a chance when moving to Brussels but is now captain of the Belgian champions and preparing to mark the England captain in the Champions League Below the zigzagging contrails that paint the blue Brussels sky, Christian Burgess is reflecting on the latest chapters of his extraordinary journey, those since joining Royale Union Saint‑Gilloise almost six years ago. At the time he felt that his career was at risk of stagnating, but after rummaging Wikipedia to get a handle on the club and learning of their big ambitions, it felt a leap of faith worth taking. Even so, there is a detectable disbelief at how that decision led him to the Champions League and an unlikely reunion with Harry Kane, with whom he last duelled as an 18-year-old on trial at Tottenham more than 15 years ago. On Wednesday Union play at Bayern Munich in arguably the biggest match in their history, knowing a positive result would keep alive their chances of advancing to the knockout stage. Continue reading...

items.title: Uitblinker Cillessen geniet van bekerruzie in achtertuin
items.summary: NEC heeft zich dinsdagavond met de nodige moeite geplaatst voor de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker. Het uitgestelde duel met buurman De Treffers eindigde in een 1-2 overwinning voor het elftal van Dick Schreuder. Na afloop spraken wij met Tjeu Langeveld en uitblinker Jasper Cillessen over de burenruzie in Groesbeek.

items.title: Rondje Europa: Ajax leeft nog in CL na escape tegen Villarreal
items.summary: Het moest van diep komen, maar Ajax maakt door een late overwinning op Villarreal nog altijd kans om door te gaan in de Champions League. In Rondje Europa blikken we woensdag uitgebreid terug op de zege van de Amsterdammers. Ook PSV komt aan bod.

items.title: Audi boss expects ‘very bumpy’ debut season despite 2030 title target
items.summary: Audi chief Mattia Binotto has warned that the new Formula 1 team could experience a "very bumpy" debut campaign despite the German brand's bosses setting the lofty target of fighting for championships by 2030.

items.title: Rose's amazing albatross helps beat Tiger's team in TGL
items.summary: Justin Rose fired the first albatross in TGL history to help Los Angeles Golf Club to an 8-4 victory over Tiger Woods’ Jupiter Links Golf Club at the SoFi Center.

items.title: Littler's birthday quiz! Test your knowledge as 'The Nuke' turns 19
items.summary: Luke Littler turns 19 on Wednesday, having already achieved more in his teenage years than almost every other darts player that came before him.

items.title: Are the Seahawks now favourite to win the Super Bowl?
items.summary: Super Bowl 60 awaits on February 8 at Levi's Stadium, but the NFL's main event will come this weekend, believes Sky Sports NFL's Jeff Reinebold.

items.title: Bellingham on drinking celeb: 'I know the truth'
items.summary: Jude Bellingham described his drinking gesture goal celebration as "a bit of a joke" aimed at speculation about his off-the-field behaviour.

items.title: Wrexham rue 'nothing moment' in Leicester draw
items.summary: Wrexham boss Phil Parkinson was left hurting after watching Jannik Vestergaard snatch a point for Leicester in their 1-1 draw but felt there were positives to take from a battling performance in horrible conditions.

items.title: Tiger Woods stunned as Max Homa almost dunks tee-shot!
items.summary: Max Homa came agonisingly close to a hole-in-one during the TGL match between Jupiter Links and LA Golf Club

items.title: 'Good signs' or just buying time? Spurs remain divided on Frank future
items.summary: Many Tottenham fans have already lost faith in Thomas Frank - but did Tuesday's 2-0 win over Borussia Dortmund give cause for optimism?

items.title: Between Gabriel Jesus and Gyökeres, Arsenal's striker battle is heating up
items.summary: Gabriel Jesus and Viktor Gyökeres each staked their claim to Arsenal's No. 9 spot in their 3-1 win over Inter Milan.

items.title: Vinícius Júnior roars back into form, much to the Bernabéu's delight
items.summary: It's been a rollercoaster of a week, but a convincing win over Monaco has gotten Real Madrid back on track.

items.title: Bellingham responds to partying claims with 'joke' drinking celebration
items.summary: Jude Bellingham responds to partying claims with a goal celebration in the Champions League win over Monaco.

items.title: 'Tears in my eyes' - Jesus enjoys 'dream night' in San Siro
items.summary: Gabriel Jesus says he had "tears in my eyes" after scoring twice to help Arsenal win 3-1 at Inter Milan for a seventh successive win in the Champions League.

items.title: Gabriel Jesus fulfils ‘dream’ at Inter as Arsenal celebrate qualification in style
items.summary: Mission accomplished for Arsenal. A seventh win out of seven ensured Mikel Arteta’s side will head straight into the last 16 of this competition as one of the two top seeds after Gabriel Jesus scored twice – including their 19th goal of the season from a corner – to see off last year’s beaten finalists. It means that as well as getting one back over an Inter team that they lost to 14 months ago, Arsenal have surpassed their longest winning streak at this level. While Manchester City’s surprise defeat in Norway in the earlier kick-off had removed any jeopardy about them progressing, this was more evidence of the ruthless streak Arsenal have developed under Arteta. The only blot on the copybook in a fourth successive away game in four different competitions was Petar Sucic’s equaliser in the first half after Jesus had given Arsenal an early lead, although this was all about the Brazil striker even after the substitute Viktor Gyökeres sealed the points late on with a classy finish. Continue reading...
